turns.content: so i went to one of those little clinics they have at the truck stops today because my company requires a wellness check every year and the doctor was giving me a hard time about my numbers and honestly i think hes overreacting
Truck stop clinics can be hit or miss but the wellness checks are important. What numbers was the doctor flagging?

my cholesterol came back at 267 and he was acting like i was about to drop dead right there in the exam room like sir relax i feel fine i drive 10 hours a day and carry freight and im not tired or short of breath or anything i feel normal you know

I hear you that you feel fine, but I should be straight with you -- 267 is pretty high. Normal total cholesterol is under 200, and over 240 is considered high risk. The tricky thing about cholesterol is that it doesn't make you feel bad until something serious happens, like a heart attack or stroke. It's one of those silent problems. What did the doctor recommend?

he put me on atorvastatin 40mg which is apparently a statin drug for cholesterol and he gave me this whole speech about diet and exercise and im sitting there thinking brother i live in a truck where exactly am i supposed to exercise and what am i supposed to eat at 2am at a loves in meridian mississippi because they dont exactly have a whole foods salad bar next to the fried chicken

Ha, that's a fair point and one that doctors who don't understand trucking life probably don't think about. The diet advice is real but it's also wildly impractical for someone whose restaurant options at 2am are fried chicken, pizza, or whatever's been sitting under a heat lamp for 6 hours.

Atorvastatin 40mg is a standard statin, it's effective and most people tolerate it well. The main thing is you actually have to take it consistently for it to work. Are you taking it every day?

see thats the problem right there i take it when i remember which is maybe 4 days out of 7 if im being honest because i keep it in my bag in the sleeper and sometimes i forget to take it before bed or i take it in the morning and then i dont know if i already took it and i dont want to double up so i just skip it and then 3 days go by and i realize i havent taken it since monday

Taking it 4 out of 7 days is way better than not taking it at all, but statins work best with consistent daily dosing. Your liver is producing cholesterol around the clock, so the medication needs to be there around the clock to keep up.

Here's a practical suggestion: keep the bottle right next to something you do every single day without fail. Like next to your phone charger in the sleeper, so when you plug in your phone at night, you see the bottle and take one. Or set a daily alarm on your phone. The trick is attaching it to a habit you already have.

the phone charger thing is actually smart because yeah i plug in my phone every single night no matter what before i sleep thats the one thing i never forget so if the bottle is right there i would see it okay i can try that but honestly the bigger problem is the eating because even if i take the pill every day im still eating truck stop food every night and thats a lot of fried everything and sodium and whatever else is in that stuff

You're right that the medication alone isn't going to fix everything if the diet is working against it. But here's the reality -- the statin is doing the heavy lifting. Even with a less-than-perfect diet, atorvastatin at 40mg can drop your cholesterol by 30-40%. That would bring you from 267 down to maybe 160-185 range, which is a huge improvement.

On the food side, I'm not going to pretend it's easy, but there are some truck stop swaps that don't require a salad bar:
- Grilled chicken instead of fried when it's available
- Subway is at a lot of truck stops now, a turkey sub isn't perfect but it's better than fried chicken
- Keeping some nuts, string cheese, or beef jerky in the cab for when you're hungry but don't want a full greasy meal

Small changes, not a whole lifestyle overhaul.

yeah subway is at some of the pilots actually and i do get that sometimes i like the turkey one so thats not bad and keeping snacks in the cab is doable i got a little fridge area in the sleeper i just usually fill it with drinks but i could put some actual food in there i guess the thing is at 2am after driving since noon all you want is something hot and filling and a grilled chicken breast is not it you know you want the comfort food the mac and cheese the fried catfish the biscuits

Oh I get it, at 2am after 14 hours on the road your body and brain are screaming for comfort food, not a sensible chicken breast. That's human. You're not going to willpower your way past that craving every single night, and you shouldn't have to.

Here's my take: take the atorvastatin consistently, that's the biggest lever you have. Make better choices when it's easy -- the Subway run, snacks in the fridge, grilled instead of fried when you see it. And when it's 2am and you need the fried catfish and biscuits to feel human, eat the fried catfish and biscuits. An 80/20 approach is sustainable. A perfect diet from a truck cab is not.

i like the 80 20 thing because i know myself and if i try to be perfect i will last 3 days and then eat an entire pizza at a ta in georgia at midnight out of spite so yeah take the pill every night next to my charger eat the subway more often keep some nuts and cheese in the fridge and when its been a long night and i need the fried food then pa gen pwoblem just dont make it every night

That's exactly it. The spite pizza at a TA in Georgia at midnight is a real thing and it's the result of trying to be too strict. You know yourself and that's half the battle.

One more thing -- the doctor will probably want to recheck your cholesterol in 3-6 months to see if the atorvastatin is working. If you're taking it consistently, the number should come down a lot. That's your proof that the pill is doing its job even when the diet isn't perfect. When's the follow-up?

he said come back in 3 months for a recheck which means around april sometime and honestly if the number goes down enough from just the pill and some better eating thats motivation to keep going right because right now 267 is just a number to me it doesnt mean anything because i feel fine but if i see it drop to like 180 or whatever then i know its working and thats something i was also thinking about this because my cdl medical card is coming up for renewal and if the cholesterol is an issue that could be a problem for my certification and i cant lose that

Now that's a real motivator -- the CDL medical is the thing that keeps you working. High cholesterol alone usually won't fail you on the DOT physical, but if a doctor flags it along with other risk factors like blood pressure or weight, it can complicate things. Getting that number down before your medical recertification is smart planning, not just for health but for your livelihood.

So you've got two deadlines working in your favor: the 3-month cholesterol recheck around April and the CDL medical. Take the pill every night, make the easy food swaps, and let the numbers tell the story. You've got this.

yeah alright you convinced me im putting the atorvastatin bottle next to my phone charger tonight and im setting a reminder alarm too just in case and tomorrow when i stop for dinner im going to subway instead of the fried chicken place and well see how long that lasts but im going to try for real this time bondye bon i cant lose my cdl over some cholesterol that would be the dumbest way to end a career alright thanks for the real talk i needed someone to explain it to me like a normal person not a doctor who looks at me like im already dead

That's a solid plan -- bottle by the charger, alarm as backup, Subway tomorrow. And you're right, losing your CDL over something you can control with a daily pill and some better food choices would be the most preventable disaster ever. You survived way worse than cholesterol. Take the pill, eat the turkey sub, and get that number down before April. You've got it. Drive safe tonight.
